response: Japan is a major global economy with a population of approximately 125 million people, generating a robust GDP of $4.2 trillion. Despite steady growth of just 1.1%, the country maintains a relatively low unemployment rate of 2.6%, though inflation stands at 3.2%, reflecting ongoing economic challenges. Japan’s high debt-to-GDP ratio of 263% contrasts with its impressive longevity, as life expectancy stands at 84.6 years, while its exports—valued at $756 billion—play a crucial role in sustaining its economic influence.
